fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Kansas Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Kansas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity kansas, how to transfer dietitian license to kansas, dietitian compact kansas, kansas dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Health Occupations Credentialing
board.parent_dept: Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services (KDADS)
board.mailing_address:
Kansas Board of Healing Arts
800 SW Jackson, Lower Level - Suite A
Topeka, KS 66612

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: The credential to practice dietetics in Kansas, regulated by the KDADS Health Occupations Credentialing division.

license_types.title: Temporary Licensed Dietitian
license_types.description: Available for applicants who have met all requirements except passing the exam. Valid for six months and can be renewed once. Temporary fee is $70.

steps.title: Create Account & Complete Online Application
steps.description: Navigate to The Board's Online Services portal at https://www.accesskansas.org/bha/. Create a new user account if you don't have one, then select the Registered Dietitian application, and complete all required sections online, including uploading a digital photograph.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable $150 application fee through the online portal as part of your application submission.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official academic transcripts to be sent directly from your college or university to the Kansas Board of Healing Arts. Electronic submission is preferred.

steps.title: Verify CDR Registration
steps.description: Contact the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) to request that your verification statement of registration be sent directly to the Kansas Board of Healing Arts.

steps.title: Complete Criminal History Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided within the online application to complete the required fingerprint-based criminal history background check. This typically involves submitting fingerprints through a designated service.

steps.title: Submit Verification of Licensure (If Applicable)
steps.description: If you hold or have held a dietitian license in another state, complete the Verification of Licensure form provided within the online application, or arrange for verification to be sent directly from the issuing board.

documents.detail: The original downloaded application form with a notarized signature.
A copy of your active CDR card to document exam and training completion.
Sent directly from the university showing your conferred degree.

provisional_text: Kansas offers a Temporary License for $70 for individuals who have fulfilled the educational and supervised practice requirements but have not yet passed the CDR exam. A temporary license allows the individual to practice under supervision for six months and may be renewed one time.
reciprocity_text: Kansas allows for licensure by reciprocity. Out-of-state applicants with a current, valid license from a state with equivalent or stricter standards, or holding active CDR registration, can apply using the standard application process to establish Kansas credentials.

faqs.question: How much does a Kansas dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial license application fee is $140. A temporary license costs $70.

faqs.question: When does the Kansas license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Kansas regular dietitian licenses are renewed biennially (every two years). The online renewal system opens annually on January 15th for those due. The renewal fee is $135.

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements for Kansas?
faqs.answer: Kansas requires a minimum of 15 hours of Continuing Professional Education (CPE) every two years. Holding current CDR registration (which requires 75 CPEs every 5 years) generally satisfies this requirement.

faqs.question: Does Kansas have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Kansas. To work in Kansas, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Kansas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 3 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Kansas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Kansas allows for licensure by reciprocity. Out-of-state applicants with a current, valid license from a state with equivalent or stricter standards, or holding active CDR registration, can apply using the standard application process to establish Kansas credentials.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Kansas
hero.dek: To apply for a Registered Dietitian license in Kansas, prospective licensees must submit an online application through The Board's Online Services portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the portal, click 'Access Online Services', then 'Online Applications'. Select 'Registered Dietitian' and click 'Start Application'. Choose 'New Applicant' and follow the prompts to create an account and begin the application.
